title: Xenial Moroccan Lamb Harira
description: A traditional Moroccan stew made with lamb mince and served in a salt block vessel, this dish is a hearty and flavorful representation of Mediterranean cuisine, featuring a rich and indulgent broth with a yeasty and molten texture.
instructions: Heat the lamb mince in a large pot over medium heat, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the chickpeas, diced tomatoes, beef broth, salt, black pepper, and paprika, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the soup has thickened slightly.
Stir in the yeast and cook for an additional 5 minutes, or until the soup has a yeasty and molten texture.
Preheat the salt block vessel in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Carefully remove the salt block from the oven and pour the harira into it.
Serve the harira hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
